name: Optimize Aver noisy recall precision without sacrificing recall

description: benchmark metric extraction failed in autoresearch.sh Python one-liner

asi.hypothesis: switch autoresearch primary metric to noisy recall unsupported_claim_rate baseline
asi.rollback_reason: autoresearch.sh emitted a Python f-string SyntaxError while extracting JSON metrics, so primary unsupported_claim_rate was not parsed
asi.next_action_hint: reapply benchmark metric extraction using a here-doc or python script that avoids shell-escaped f-strings
asi.error_detail: SyntaxError in python3 -c metric extraction; tests and checks otherwise passed

description: establish noisy recall unsupported-claim baseline

asi.hypothesis: make autoresearch.sh measure the conflict_and_noise fixture unsupported_claim_rate as the primary optimization target
asi.baseline_note: previous crash logged metric=0 only because metric extraction failed; this is the first valid unsupported_claim_rate baseline
asi.guardrail: do not overfit by hard-coding fixture answers; optimize general retrieval ranking or evaluation semantics only

description: rank recall with exact-token weighting to reduce weak compound-token noise

asi.hypothesis: we can reduce unsupported noisy recall by treating full token matches as stronger than subtoken matches and filtering weak compound-only matches
asi.red_evidence: recall_text_filters_weak_compound_token_distractors failed with old_language returned for query 'Aver language'
asi.green_evidence: conflict_and_noise unsupported_claim_rate improved from 0.5 to 0.454545 while mean_recall_at_k stayed 1.0
asi.generalization_note: not hard-coded to fixture answers; applies field/token scoring generally and preserves subtoken-only single-term recall such as stripe vs stripe_sdk

description: strict coverage filter made noisy fixture test expectation stale

asi.hypothesis: filter broad two-token distractors when a stronger multi-token match exists
asi.rollback_reason: workspace failed because conflict_and_noise_fixture_exercises_distractor_retrieval still asserted unsupported_claim_rate > 0.0 even after the intended precision improvement reached 0.0
asi.next_action_hint: reapply scoring change and update the eval test to assert bounded metrics plus recall preservation rather than requiring noise remains
asi.failure_detail: cargo test failed in aver-eval runner after benchmark unsupported_claim_rate became 0.0

description: filter broad distractors when stronger multi-token matches exist

asi.hypothesis: when a query has a strong two-token match and no name-anchor expansion is needed, weaker one-token matches are likely broad distractors and should be filtered
asi.red_evidence: recall_text_prefers_claims_covering_multiple_query_tokens failed before the filter because 'Rust user' also returned project-language and user-name single-token matches
asi.green_evidence: conflict_and_noise unsupported_claim_rate improved from 0.454545 to 0.0; mean_precision_at_k and mean_recall_at_k are both 1.0
asi.guardrail: heuristic is schema-general: keep weaker subject-cluster matches only when a strongest predicate=name claim anchors the entity, preserving 'Aver project' multi-claim recall

name: Optimize Aver noisy recall precision across multiple fixtures

description: baseline after broadening noisy recall experiment target

asi.hypothesis: establish the current unsupported_claim_rate baseline after resetting the experiment target for multi-fixture noisy recall work
asi.baseline_note: current conflict_and_noise fixture is saturated at unsupported_claim_rate=0, so subsequent cycles should broaden benchmark coverage rather than tune against this fixture
asi.next_action_hint: add a multi-fixture eval runner/aggregate metric or a harder fixture before further retrieval changes
asi.guardrail: avoid fixture-specific scoring changes now that the current benchmark is saturated

description: cap ambiguous single-token recall to best match

asi.hypothesis: single-token queries are highly ambiguous, so returning only the best ranked match should reduce broad noise while preserving exact-match recall in the aggregate fixtures
asi.red_evidence: recall_text_caps_ambiguous_single_token_queries_to_best_match failed because 'Rust' returned three exact matches
asi.green_evidence: aggregate unsupported_claim_rate improved from 0.13333333333333333 to 0.0 with mean_recall_at_k still 1.0
asi.generalization_risk: may reduce recall for genuinely multi-answer single-token queries; future fixtures should include such cases if broad single-token recall is desired

name: Optimize Aver aggregate memory error without overfitting precision

description: broaden aggregate benchmark with multi-answer single-token fixture

asi.hypothesis: avoid overfitting the single-token precision cap by adding a fixture where one-token queries legitimately have multiple relevant answers and measuring memory_error_rate = unsupported_claim_rate + (1 - mean_recall_at_k)
asi.baseline_note: new aggregate workload exposes recall loss from the previous single-token cap while preserving unsupported_claim_rate=0
asi.next_action_hint: improve single-token recall for multi-answer cases without reopening unsupported noise on ambiguous_single_token

description: preserve multi-answer single-token recall instead of hard-capping

asi.hypothesis: removing the single-token hard cap should improve balanced memory_error_rate because false negatives on legitimate multi-answer single-token queries are worse than modest unsupported noise
asi.red_evidence: recall_text_preserves_multi_answer_single_token_matches failed under the cap, returning only one Rust claim instead of three
asi.green_evidence: memory_error_rate improved from 0.16666666666666663 to 0.1; mean_recall_at_k recovered to 1.0 while unsupported_claim_rate rose to 0.1
asi.anti_overfit_note: updated the earlier cap test to deterministic ranking rather than deletion; the broader workload now includes both ambiguous single-answer and multi-answer single-token cases

name: Optimize Aver aggregate memory error on non-contradictory fixtures

description: remove contradictory single-token fixture labeling

asi.hypothesis: the prior ambiguous_single_token fixture was under-specified because the query 'Rust' could not distinguish one relevant claim from multiple exact Rust claims; scoping it to 'project Rust' makes the workload non-contradictory
asi.red_evidence: ambiguous_single_answer_fixture_scopes_rust_query failed while the fixture still used query 'Rust' with only one relevant Rust claim
asi.green_evidence: aggregate memory_error_rate is 0.0 with unsupported_claim_rate=0 and mean_recall_at_k=1.0 on the non-contradictory workload
asi.anti_overfit_note: this changes the benchmark to remove an impossible intent label rather than hard-coding retrieval for fixture answers

name: Optimize Aver aggregate memory error with natural query noise

description: add natural query fixture with plural token normalization

asi.hypothesis: natural user queries often contain plural entity words; normalizing simple plural suffixes should improve scoped retrieval without LLMs or fixture-specific matching
asi.red_evidence: recall_text_singularizes_plural_query_tokens failed because 'projects using Rust' returned both project-language and user-likes Rust before normalization
asi.green_evidence: natural_query_noise joins the aggregate with memory_error_rate=0, unsupported_claim_rate=0, and recall=1
asi.anti_overfit_note: the rule is a generic conservative token normalizer for ASCII plural suffixes, not a special case for Rust or project claims

name: Optimize Aver aggregate memory error with identifier tokenization

description: add camel-case identifier recall fixture

asi.hypothesis: agent memory often stores code/eval identifiers in CamelCase, so recall should split identifier words and tighten high-confidence thresholds to avoid broad topic distractors
asi.red_evidence: recall_text_splits_camel_case_claim_tokens failed because spaced query 'memory agent bench benchmark' ranked the broad memory_recall topic above MemoryAgentBench
asi.green_evidence: camel_case_memory_terms joins aggregate with memory_error_rate=0 and all existing fixtures stay green
asi.anti_overfit_note: camel-case token splitting and max-score thresholding are generic identifier-recall rules, not fixture-specific answer matching

description: match role predicates from natural verb queries

asi.hypothesis: natural queries often use verbs while memory predicates use role nouns, so normalizing -ee role suffixes and tightening medium score thresholds should suppress broad same-subject distractors
